title: A Path to Industry 5.0 Digital Twins for Human–Robot Collaboration by Bridging NEP+ and ROS
summary: New article in 'Robotics' introduces the nep2ros ROS package, designed to bridge the Robot Operating System (ROS) with the cross-platform NEP+ framework to facilitate Industry 5.0 human digital twins.
description1: Aligning with the Industry 5.0 vision, this framework places humanity at the center by empowering industrial workers and establishing inclusive and flexible production processes.
description2: The nep2ros package harmonizes the techno-centered focus of ROS with the platform-independent middleware of NEP+, enabling bidirectional data exchange between diverse hardware and software landscapes.
highlight: This initiative facilitates diverse development scenarios beyond conventional robotics, underpinning a transformative shift in Industry 5.0 applications by amplifying accessibility and utility across diverse platforms.
description3: The framework's usability is demonstrated through an immersive, human-in-the-loop collaborative assembly scenario involving a UR5e industrial robot and an Oculus Quest 2 VR headset.
